channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Keeping cool: How French cities are adapting to extreme heat
item.description: If you've spent any time in France recently, you'll have noticed it has got warmer, especially in French cities. How is climate change impacting France? Why do cities suffer more and what are big urban areas doing to combat the effects of climate change? Also, why are the French so resistant to air conditioning and how do people keep cool during a heatwave? We take a closer look in this edition of French Connections.
item.dc:creator: Florence VILLEMINOT

item.title: Health awareness fuels boom of traditional cuisine in Zimbabwe
item.description: Traditional foods are making a strong comeback in Zimbabwe's second-largest city. Growing health awareness and nostalgia are fuelling demand for indigenous dishes. Restaurants serving traditional dishes are multiplying and attracting everyone from healthy diners to Zimbabweans coming home from abroad.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Harry Kane Shines For England As Ronaldo Misfires
item.description: The Three Lions began their World Cup campaign with an upbeat win against Croatia. Thomas Tuchel's tactics saw them get ahead and win 4-2 after the two sides were level at halftime. Meanwhile Portugal were held to a 1-1 draw by the Democratic Republic of Congo in the latest upset of a team considered to be among the favourites to win the tournament. Cristiano Ronaldo didn't get a shot on target and questions are being raised over how much time he should be given in games.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: Benin's 'model husbands' school educates on gender relations
item.description: Men in northern Benin are being forced to face the uncomfortable realities of women who love them and live with them. A training programme is raising awareness about men can make a huge difference in alleviating the health risks and social pressures placed on women in some conservative communities. Graduates of the course become advocates themselves and have even been dubbed "model husbands". FRANCE 24's correspondent in Benin tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At G7 summit, Kenya calls for better recognition of Africa by international institutions
item.description: Kenya's President William Ruto attended the G7 summit after its host, French President Emmanuel Macron, invited him during the Africa Forward summit held in Nairobi a few weeks earlier. Ruto used the platform to advocate for Africa to be better recognised by international institutions. FRANCE 24's Bastien Renouil tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: EY's Jad Shimaly on how AI is reshaping business and the workplace
item.description: From boosting productivity to transforming recruitment and consulting, artificial intelligence is changing the way companies operate. Speaking to FRANCE 24 at VivaTech in Paris, EY executive Jad Shimaly explains where AI is delivering results and what it could mean for the future of work.
item.dc:creator: Charles PELLEGRIN

item.title: Poland detains suspect in fatal shooting of Russian artist critical of Putin, Tusk says
item.description: Polish police on Thursday detained a suspect in the daylight killing of a Russian artist known as ‌Semyon Skrepetsky, who was known for his unsparing caricatures of prominent political figures in the Kremlin. Prime Minister Donald Tusk said the man was using a Georgian passport.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Egypt grapples with video game addiction
item.description: Gaming cafés have become a common sight on the streets of Cairo, especially in poorer neighbourhoods. Egyptians hold a world record for time spent gaming per day, with an average of 1 hour and 43 minutes daily. Some 43.5 percent of young gamers in Egypt are considered to be addicted. In February, the government launched a campaign to combat addiction. FRANCE 24's Mathilde Delvigne reports.
item.dc:creator: Mathilde DELVIGNE

item.title: Sudanese refugees abused and exploited by MSF staff in Chad, says report
item.description: Dozens of Doctors Without Borders staff have been accused of sexually abusing Sudanese refugees in Chad, adding that it dismissed 18 workers after investigations found "serious misconduct". A report accused staff members of targeting underaged girls and trading food or jobs for sex.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran-US: Donald Trump signs peace memorandum in Versailles
item.description: Donald Trump put his signature to the memorandum of understanding during a candlelit dinner at the Palace of Versailles following a G7 summit.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup favourites' competition starts bring very different results
item.description: Two favourites made their entry into the World Cup on June 18, with two very different results. While Portugal did less well than expected, drawing with DR Congo, England had a great start, beating Croatia 4 to 2 and cementing its status as favourite of the competition.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Tehran comes out as the 'winner' of the initial US - Iran deal
item.description: The US and Iran have signed an agreement paving the way to an end of the war between the two countries and starting a 60-day negotiating clock to reach a final deal. Although US President Donald Trump has hailed the deal a success, it has been met with criticism as many denounce concessions made to Tehran. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Starmer rival seeks win in UK by-election pivotal to prime minister's fate
item.description: The UK's Prime Minister Keir Starmer has insisted he will fight to stay in his role if a leadership challenge is launched by Andy Burnham. But before any such contest unfolds, Burnham must win a seat in parliament, which he is hoping to do on June 18 in a by-election in the Makerfield constituency. The race is expected to be tight, with Reform UK candidate Rob Kenyon posing a credible opposition to the former Greater Manchester Mayor.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Catastrophic capitulation': Why US and Israel are the biggest losers in Iran deal
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, June 18: An Israeli paper slams the US's deal with Iran as a "catastrophic capitulation". While Trump sees the US as the big winner, the press says Iran's chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz shows that it has set a new status quo. Also, EU leaders scramble to find anti-China trade measures without provoking Beijing into a trade war. Plus, as a heatwave returns to Europe, French resort towns plan to fine men who go shirtless in public.
item.dc:creator: Dheepthika LAURENT

item.title: 🔴 Middle East live: Switzerland says talks between US and Iran still planned for Friday
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Climate-change driven heatwave brings uncertainty to French farmers
item.description: The sweltering heat returning to France is hitting farmers particularly hard. Temperatures are set to reach up to 40 degrees celsius , but those in the agriculture sector have no choice but to carry on with their daily work.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Starmer on the line as UK holds "most important local election in decades"
item.description: Keir Starmer isn’t on the ballot, but the U.K. prime minister’s future is on the line in a special election on June 18. Voters in the Makerfield district of northwest England are electing a new lawmaker. The leading contender is Andy Burnham, the current Labour mayor of Greater Manchester and the favourite to be the next prime minister. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot explains why.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US tells NATO it will review its force presence in Europe
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told NATO the Pentagon will conduct a review of its force presence in Europe within the next six months. The review will depend on how fast European NATO allies take responsibility for their own security. This comes on the back of a G7 summit deemed a success, as Trump has signalled backing Ukraine against Russia. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating tells us more from Brussels.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Long Island serial killer sentenced to life in prison
item.description: A chilling mystery that haunted New York for more than a decade has finally reached a definitive end. On June 17, a Long Island architect was sentenced to life in prison without parole for a horrifying string of serial killings. The "Gilgo Killer" targeted young women mostly in their 20s, who worked as online escorts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New US Fed chair vows reforms as central bank signals rate hikes on horizon
item.description: As widely expected, policymakers at the the US Federal Reserve have kept interest rates unchanged. But in a clear change of tone from previous meetings, Fed officials said they expected raising interest rates later this year rather than lowering them, to tame rising inflation. The meeting was the first chaired by the central bank's new chief, Kevin Warsh, who scrapped guidance about future rate moves and announced new task forces that could reshape how the Fed communicates and analyses data.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Mixed reactions over US-Iran deal in Lebanon
item.description: Although the US-Iran deal brings hope to Lebanon, reactions remain mixed. Lebanon may be mentioned in the first paragraph, it is not included in the $300 billion reconstruction fund despite the south of the country having been totally destroyed, and Israeli strikes have not stopped. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis tells us more about it from Beirut.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran deal 'worst policy blunder in decades': criticism spreads across US political spectrum
item.description: US President Donald Trump and his Iranian counterpart, President Masoud Pezeshkian, have both now signed the memorandum of understanding, designed to bring the conflict in the middle east to a close. The document, which consists of 14 points, has not yet been released, but officials have clarified much of what's in it. The memorandum has been met with heavy criticism back in the US, from both Democrats and Republicans. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert explains why.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US will review its military presence in Europe, Hegseth tells NATO
item.description: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth told a group of NATO defence ministers Thursday that Washington will carry out a sweeping review of its military presence in Europe. The Pentagon chief slammed some European allies for failing to provide US forces access to bases in Europe during the US-Israeli war on Iran, calling it “shameful”.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Is football coming home? English fans tell FRANCE 24
item.description: Can Team England bring the #FIFAWorldCup home, 60 years after its first did so in 1966?FRANCE 24 asks some supporters of the #ThreeLions what they think.Do you think #football is coming home? Let us know in the comments below!
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
